task_completion.description: Data points from the CSV are entered and plotted as a scatter plot in GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for multiple discrete points (up to 12) plotted in the GeoGebra graphics view, forming a shape that is initially linear and then curves upwards (x-axis is stress, y-axis is strain).

task_completion.description: Sliders for E, K, and n are created, and the Ramberg-Osgood model curve is plotted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three sliders labeled E, K, and n in the algebra or graphics view, and a continuous curve representing the function f(x) = 100*(x/E + (x/K)^n).

task_completion.description: Residual segments are created between each data point and the model curve.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for short line segments connecting the discrete data points to the continuous model curve in the graphics view.

task_completion.description: A text element displaying the sum of squared residuals is added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box in the graphics view showing a calculated value for the sum of squared residuals (e.g., 'SSR = ...' or similar).

task_completion.description: The 0.2% offset line is plotted and its intersection with the model curve is marked.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a straight line parallel to the initial linear portion of the model curve, offset by 0.2 on the y-axis. There should be a specific point marked where this offset line intersects the model curve.

task_completion.description: A text element displaying the yield stress is added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box in the graphics view explicitly stating 'Yield Stress = [value] MPa'.

task_completion.description: The model curve closely fits the data, and the yield stress value is accurate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The continuous curve should pass very close to the data points. The value shown in the 'Yield Stress' text or the x-coordinate of the intersection point should be approximately 242 MPa (acceptable range is 202 to 282 MPa).

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application interface with the algebra view, graphics view, and input bar being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent entered the actual data from the provided CSV file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The points in the algebra view or spreadsheet should match the stress-strain data from the file, rather than arbitrary or randomly placed points.

integrity.description: The yield stress is derived from the intersection point, not just typed as a hardcoded text without the geometric construction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The algebra view should show the intersection command or point definition, and the offset line should be visibly constructed, rather than just a standalone text box with the answer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true parameters for the Ramberg-Osgood model are E = 69000, K = 450, and n = 10. The yield stress (0.2% offset) is approximately 241.7 MPa. Acceptable yield stress values are between 202 and 282 MPa. The data contains 12 points. The offset line is g(x) = 100*x/E + 0.2. The x-axis represents stress (MPa) and the y-axis represents strain (%).
